content:
After rebuilding several frontend and Node.js applications, I realized the biggest improvements in JavaScript weren’t new frameworks — they were language features that quietly made my code cleaner, safer, and much easier to maintain.

6 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

JavaScript changes incredibly fast.
Every year, a new framework appears.
A new bundler becomes popular.
A new runtime promises better performance.
It’s easy to get distracted by the ecosystem.
But over the last couple of years, I found myself spending less time learning frameworks and more time exploring the language itself.
That turned out to be one of the best investments I made.
Many developers — including experienced ones — still write JavaScript the same way they did five or six years ago. Modern JavaScript has evolved significantly, and using its newer features has helped me remove boilerplate, reduce bugs, and write code that’s much easier to read.
Here are the ten features that genuinely changed how I write JavaScript in 2026.
